heaviest_rainfall.status: Ranked highest for total rainfall, today's storm had precipitation totalling 47.06mm, falling at 24.6mm per hour at times during the 142.3 hour long storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 24.6mm per hour was the peak rain rate recorded today ranking 2nd in our overall records.  The storm lasted 142.3 hours and 47.06mm of rain fell.  On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: A storm today ranked highest for the longest continuous rainfall according to our records, lasting a total of142.3 hours.  It came with 47.06mm of rainfall, at times, falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: It is currently one of the longest rainy periods.  It is the ranked 8th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been rainy starting on 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 6 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (180°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 49.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.1°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 0.1 mm) and cloud levels (up 287 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 117.5m higher than yesterday, 37.7m lower than the past week, 287.0m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 561m to 715m and averaged 729.1m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; you can expect comfortable, mild temperatures. Also, pleasant humidity levels and little or no wind. A few light raindrops may fall, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and some sunny spells will occur. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (91%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
